title: Compile and Test the Migrated Application
breadcrumbs: Compile and Test the Migrated Application
text: Compile and Test the Migrated Application Compile and test the migrated application to identify and correct possible changes in the way the application functions. Compile the application in Uniface 10.4 . Use the /all /war /cmi=1 command line switch to compile all objects, generate CMI files, and generate warnings. For more information, see /all and /cmi . For example, for Uniface 9 imports you may see extra compilation warnings because ProcScript entries are compiled differently. Check the compilation logs. Pay special attention to warnings or errors that you did not get when compiling in earlier Uniface versions. Fix problems when needed. Run the application and test it. Fix any functional issues that you discover in either Uniface 10.4 or in the version you are migrating from. Next steps: If you fix issues in your earlier version of Uniface, repeat the previous task: Migrate Repository Objects from Uniface 9 . If you fix issues in Uniface 10.4 , compile and test again.
